text.title: Indirect Prompt Injection Defense Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Indirect Prompt Injection Defense must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every produced instance carries the full template field set. Optional fields are explicitly marked 'N/A' with a reason, never silently omitted.
rule.rationale: silently-empty fields collapse into 'we forgot' and the artefact loses audit value.

rule.statement: The template is versioned (semver). Each filled instance records the template_version it used so downstream consumers can re-parse legacy artefacts.
rule.rationale: templates without version pinning become a one-way ratchet; old instances become unreadable when the template drifts.

rule.statement: Each instance carries a single named owner and a last_touched timestamp. Instances with no touch in N cycles are auto-archived, not silently rotted.
rule.rationale: un-owned artefacts metastasize and the operator stops trusting the whole library.

rule.statement: Every non-trivial field value is anchored to a verbatim citation from the source brief, transcript, ticket, or dashboard. No anchor → field is rejected as fabrication.

rationale.source: Anti-hallucination practice for LLM-agent design, evals, safety, cost

rule.rationale: LLMs cheerfully invent plausible values without inputs; verbatim anchoring makes the artefact falsifiable.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Harden an agent against prompt injection and jailbreak across tool boundaries (role: p7-llm-agent-developer) Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: >=1 verbatim citation per non-trivial field (URL, ticket id, transcript_id, metric snapshot) Decision: downstream consumer acts without re-deriving the reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger to artefact to decision — with auditability at each step. Source rationale: faion has `cheap-guardrail-tripwire` + `refusal-field-strict-schema` (output side). Missing: input-side IPI defense — trust-boundary model across tools, span-tainting, sandboxed reasoning over untrusted content, exfil canaries. The single m

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: "we should do this thing" Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: "industry best practice" with no source Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is catalogued as a failure mode in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists specifically to prevent this shape.
